---
title: "File Management and RStudio Projects"
subtitle: "EC 421: Introduction to Econometrics"
author: "Edward Rubin"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
    self-contained: true
    theme:
    - cosmo
    - ed.scss
editor:
  render-on-save: true
---

## Projects

Using RStudio projects is a great way to manage your files and keep your work organized.

A project is a folder that contains all the files related to a specific analysis or research project. When you create a project, RStudio will automatically set the working directory to the project folder, which makes it easier to manage your files and avoid confusion.


## Setting up a project

![Step 1: Click "New Project" in the "File" menu.](./images/ex1.png){.lightbox}

![Step 2: Choose "New Directory".](./images/ex2.png){.lightbox}

![Step 3: Choose "New Project".](./images/ex3.png){.lightbox}

![Step 4: Name your project and choose a location to save it.](./images/ex4.png){.lightbox}

![Notice that RStudio now shows the name of your project in the top right corner.](./images/ex5.png){.lightbox}

![Step 5: Put your data and QMD/RMD files in your project.](./images/ex6.png){.lightbox}


## Accessing projects

To access an existing project, you have two options:

![Option 1: Click "Open Project" in the "File" menu.](./images/ex8.png){.lightbox}

![Option 2: Click on the project name in the top right corner of RStudio.](./images/ex7.png){.lightbox}
